header.note: The source node in most cases contains the sample message as written by the developer. If it looks like a dot-delimitted string such as "form.label.firstname", then the developer has not provided a default message.

trans-unit.resname: Validator %validator% expects parameter %parameter% to be in type %$type%.
trans-unit.source: Validator %validator% expects parameter %parameter% to be in type %$type%.
trans-unit.target: Validator %validator% expects parameter %parameter% to be in type %$type%.
trans-unit.note: key: Validator %validator% expects parameter %parameter% to be in type %$type%.

trans-unit.resname: Validator %validator% expects parameter %parameter% to be of %type%.
trans-unit.source: Validator %validator% expects parameter %parameter% to be of %type%.
trans-unit.target: Validator %validator% expects parameter %parameter% to be of %type%.
trans-unit.note: key: Validator %validator% expects parameter %parameter% to be of %type%.

trans-unit.resname: Validator %validator% expects parameter %parameter% to be set.
trans-unit.source: Validator %validator% expects parameter %parameter% to be set.
trans-unit.target: Validator %validator% expects parameter %parameter% to be set.
trans-unit.note: key: Validator %validator% expects parameter %parameter% to be set.
